hierarchy.lvl1: Clustering
Clustering

records.content: Sub-analysis page. Components are scoped with the clustering. path prefix.

hierarchy.lvl1: Clustering
Clustering

records.content:
Clustering measures the Landy–Szalay ξ(s, μ) multipoles (ℓ = 0, 2, 4) on a
linear 4 Mpc/h s-binning from 0 to 200 Mpc/h, NGC + SGC combined. Pre-recon ξ
is computed from the raw catalogs; post-recon ξ uses the RecSym estimator with
the shifted catalogs from 

reconstruction, sliced into tracer
z-bins.

The binning, μ-binning, estimator, and ℓ range are all locked to the published
RascalC covariance grid, which is why this stage carries only a single decision
rather than a set of binning knobs.

hierarchy.lvl1: Clustering
Clustering

records.content:
The one free choice is whether to apply imaging-systematics weights. The same
RascalC covariance null-tests this toggle at 0.27 σ, so it is left unlocked:

hierarchy.lvl1: Clustering
Clustering

records.content:
Imaging-systematics weights — selected: On (default)

BAO is insensitive to the imaging-systematics correction (largest
shift 0.27 sigma across tracers). This decision exposes that null
test. on uses the full DESI WEIGHT column
(WEIGHT_COMP * WEIGHT_SYS * WEIGHT_ZFAIL). off drops the WEIGHT_SYS
factor, keeping only completeness + redshift-failure corrections.

Full WEIGHT * WEIGHT_FKP, matches DESI fiducial clustering.

WEIGHT_COMP * WEIGHT_ZFAIL * WEIGHT_FKP -- ‘No weight’ null test.

hierarchy.lvl1: Clustering
Clustering

records.content:
The headline diagnostic is the stacked ξ multipoles figure across tracers:



Figure 1:Pre- and post-recon xi_ell(s) multipoles (ell = 0, 2, 4) plotted as
s^2 * xi(s), one row per tracer so the BAO feature at s ~ 100 Mpc/h
is visible. Post-recon monopole should show a sharper BAO peak.

ASTRA provenance — xi_multipoles_plot · figure

Derived from: 16 upstream products — xi_pre_recon_bgs, xi_post_recon_bgs, xi_pre_recon_lrg1, xi_post_recon_lrg1, xi_pre_recon_lrg2, xi_post_recon_lrg2, …python -u scripts/plot_xi.py --output {output}

The full per-tracer registry — one xi_pre_recon_<tracer> and one
xi_post_recon_<tracer> per tracer — is:

Output

Type

Description

xi_pre_recon_bgs

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for BGS (z=0.1-0.4). Monopole-only fit.

xi_post_recon_bgs

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for BGS (RecSym). BGS parent recon spans 0.1 <= z <= 0.4 (no sub-binning).

xi_pre_recon_lrg1

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for LRG1. pycorr TwoPointCorrelationFunction (.npy),
GCcomb, s in [0, 200] Mpc/h at 4 Mpc/h bins, mu in [-1, 1] at 200 bins.

xi_post_recon_lrg1

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for LRG1 (RecSym). Slices the LRG parent post-recon
catalog to 0.4 <= z <= 0.6.

xi_pre_recon_lrg2

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for LRG2. Same format as xi_pre_recon_lrg1.

xi_post_recon_lrg2

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for LRG2 (RecSym). Slices LRG parent NPZ to 0.6 <= z <= 0.8.

xi_pre_recon_lrg3

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for LRG3 (z=0.8-1.1). Same format as xi_pre_recon_lrg1.

xi_post_recon_lrg3

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for LRG3 (RecSym). Slices LRG parent NPZ to 0.8 <= z <= 1.1.

xi_pre_recon_elg1

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for ELG1 (z=0.8-1.1). Monopole-only fit.

xi_post_recon_elg1

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for ELG1 (RecSym). Slices ELG parent NPZ to 0.8 <= z <= 1.1.

xi_pre_recon_elg2

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for ELG2 (z=1.1-1.6). Same format as xi_pre_recon_lrg1.

xi_post_recon_elg2

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for ELG2 (RecSym). Slices ELG parent NPZ to 1.1 <= z <= 1.6.

xi_pre_recon_lrg3_elg1

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for the LRG3+ELG1 combined tracer (0.8 < z < 1.1).

xi_post_recon_lrg3_elg1

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for the LRG3+ELG1 combined tracer (RecSym).

xi_pre_recon_qso

data

Pre-recon xi(s, mu) for QSO (z=0.8-2.1). Monopole-only fit.

xi_post_recon_qso

data

Post-recon xi(s, mu) for QSO (RecSym, sm30). QSO parent recon spans
0.8 <= z <= 2.1 (no sub-binning) with Sigma_sm = 30 h^-1 Mpc — matches
the sm30 RascalC cov.

xi_multipoles_plot

figure

Pre- and post-recon xi_ell(s) multipoles (ell = 0, 2, 4) plotted as
s^2 * xi(s), one row per tracer so the BAO feature at s ~ 100 Mpc/h
is visible. Post-recon monopole should show a sharper BAO peak.

Seo & Eisenstein (2007)

Planck Collaboration et al. (2018)

Padmanabhan et al. (2008)

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Every italic-underlined term below is a live ASTRA object: hover a


BAO detected post-recon finding or a


Covariance matrix source decision for its full record, and the
figures carry their own provenance. The prose is the author’s; the evidence
is pulled from astra.yaml and the materialised result products.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
We reproduce the DESI DR1 configuration-space baryon-acoustic-oscillation (BAO)
measurement across all tracer–redshift bins — BGS, LRG1, LRG2, LRG3, ELG1,
ELG2, the combined LRG3+ELG1 bin, and QSO — by fitting a damped-BAO template to
the two-point correlation function \xi(s) before and after density-field
reconstruction. Working in the \alpha_\mathrm{iso}–\alpha_\mathrm{AP} basis
(with \alpha_\mathrm{iso} alone for the 1D tracers BGS, ELG1, QSO), the
baseline configuration recovers a well-defined acoustic feature in every
post-reconstruction tracer and sharply tightens the isotropic dilation: for
ELG1, \sigma(\alpha_\mathrm{iso}) falls from


0.0696 to


0.0207
post-reconstruction. The strongest single constraint, the combined LRG3+ELG1
bin, reaches \alpha_\mathrm{iso} =


0.9995 ± 0.0088.
Propagated to distances, we obtain D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d, and D_V/r_d at the
percent level per tracer — for LRG3+ELG1, D_V/r_d =


19.88 ± 0.17 at
z_\mathrm{eff} = 

0.93 —
consistent with the Planck 2018 \LambdaCDM standard ruler.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
The BAO feature imprinted in the galaxy correlation function at the
sound-horizon scale r_d is the most robust standard ruler in large-scale
structure. Its utility is limited by non-linear evolution,
which smears the acoustic peak and degrades the achievable distance precision by
a factor of a few — a 

few-fold precision loss that
density-field reconstruction was introduced to recover. We measure the BAO scale
in DESI DR1 in configuration space, both before and after reconstruction, and
propagate the result to cosmological distances. Fourier-space P(k) is out of
scope.

The headline result is twofold: the acoustic feature is


BAO detected post-recon in every tracer after reconstruction,
and reconstruction 

Reconstruction tightens α_iso — delivering


~1 % α_iso precision for the best-measured LRG bins.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
The measurement consumes three input classes: the raw DESI DR1 LSS clustering
catalogs (galaxies plus eighteen random realisations per cap), the tabulated
fiducial cosmology supplying z \to r(z), and the RascalC analytic covariance
matrices — one pre- and one post-reconstruction file per tracer. The covariances
are ingested as published rather than recomputed, which fixes the \xi(s)
binning to the published linear grid and, as
discussed below, pins several upstream choices to their fiducial settings. The
full input register is tabulated in the 

analysis record.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
The pipeline runs in two upstream stages — 

Reconstruction
then 

Clustering — feeding a template-fitting stage that
produces one MCMC chain per (tracer, reconstruction state).

Reconstruction. The linear displacement field is estimated from the
Gaussian-smoothed density and applied symmetrically to galaxies and randoms
(the 

convention choice, RecSym), which sharpens the acoustic
peak. The single load-bearing knob is the 

Reconstruction Gaussian smoothing scale (BGS/LRG/ELG):
it sets the \Sigma_\mathrm{sm} entering the displacement solve, and because the
same scale re-enters the BAO damping template downstream, the reconstruction
stage inherits it from the root rather than choosing independently.

Clustering. We measure the Landy–Szalay \xi(s,\mu) multipoles on the raw
and shifted catalogs. Because the covariance is pinned to the published RascalC
grid (the 

Covariance matrix source decision), the binning and
estimator are locked to that grid and the non-fiducial smoothing options are
declared incompatible with it — so the baseline universe validates only at
fiducial smoothing. The one remaining free choice,


imaging weights, is null-tested by the same covariance at
sub-\sigma significance and left unlocked.

Template fitting. Each chain fits the fiducial damped-BAO template with
desilike + emcee. The 2D tracers carry \alpha_\mathrm{iso} and
\alpha_\mathrm{AP} (with D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d derived in-chain); the 1D tracers
fit \alpha_\mathrm{iso} only. The dominant modelling dial is the


Broadband model model — the spline form is fiducial and has been
shown to 

give α consistent with the polynomial form, with the residual
absorbed into the modelling-error budget. BAO damping is controlled jointly by
the 

BAO damping parameter prior and 

Damping prior central values,
anchored to the result that 

mis-centred damping priors bias α.
Three further template-shape choices — 

FoG damping placement,


Component that α dilates, and 

Wiggle / no-wiggle split
— are pinned to fiducial and exposed to document the modelling-systematic
budget they underlie; the fiducial dilation acts on the wiggle component, the
expected behaviour given that reconstruction 

reduces the non-linear BAO damping.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
The isolated BAO feature, fit per tracer pre and post-reconstruction, is shown
in 

Figure 1. The peak 

BAO peak sharpens,
and the corresponding \Delta\chi^2(\alpha_\mathrm{iso}) profiles against a
no-BAO reference (

Figure 2) develop a well-defined minimum
near the fiducial scale in every post-reconstruction tracer.



Figure 1:Isolated BAO feature + best-fit xi model, pre vs post, one row per tracer.

ASTRA provenance — bao_fit_plot · figure

Derived from: 8 upstream products — bao_fit_isolated_bgs, bao_fit_isolated_lrg1, bao_fit_isolated_lrg2, bao_fit_isolated_lrg3, bao_fit_isolated_elg1, bao_fit_isolated_elg2, …python -u scripts/plot_bao_fit.py --output {output}



Figure 2:delta-chi2 vs alpha_iso for BAO vs noBAO fits; detection significance per tracer.

ASTRA provenance — bao_detection_plot · figure

Derived from: 8 upstream products — bao_detection_chi2_bgs, bao_detection_chi2_lrg1, bao_detection_chi2_lrg2, bao_detection_chi2_lrg3, bao_detection_chi2_elg1, bao_detection_chi2_elg2, …python -u scripts/plot_bao_detection.py --output {output}

records.content:
Condensing the chains, the post-reconstruction isotropic dilation is consistent
with unity across the suite. The strongest 2D bins reach the sub-percent regime:
\alpha_\mathrm{iso} =


0.9995 ± 0.0088
for the combined LRG3+ELG1 bin and


1.003 ± 0.0097
for LRG3. The gain from reconstruction is largest where non-linear evolution is
worst — for ELG1, \sigma(\alpha_\mathrm{iso}) contracts from


0.0696 to


0.0207, and for
LRG2 from 

0.0195
to 

0.0114. The
full \alpha_\mathrm{iso}/\alpha_\mathrm{AP} table, with the \chi^2 and
degrees of freedom per fit, is:

of freedom; QSO is the least well-behaved, at \chi^2 =

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
The reported uncertainties fold a combined modelling + HOD + fiducial-cosmology
systematic budget into the chains at the aggregation step, toggled by the


Reported error includes modelling systematic decision as a table rebuild rather
than a refit. The budget is anchored to the DESI companion modelling result that


sets the combined systematic budget, dominated by the wiggle/no-wiggle
split, FoG placement, and template-dilation choices catalogued in the decision
register. Fiducial-cosmology dependence is swept through


BAO template cosmology (a set of AbacusSummit grids), and the
remaining 

Fit range in s, 

Multipoles fit (LRG only; 1D tracers override), and


BAO fit method decisions exist for sensitivity tests; none
moves the baseline result beyond its quoted budget.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
A configuration-space BAO analysis of DESI DR1 detects the acoustic feature in
all eight tracer bins post-reconstruction, reaches sub-percent isotropic
precision in the strongest LRG bins, and yields a self-consistent set of
D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d, D_V/r_d distances at the percent level — reproducing
the DESI DR1 BAO distance ladder. Every numerical claim above is traceable to a
registered ASTRA finding, the decision that configured it, and the materialised
output product it summarises, catalogued below.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
The article’s claims hyperlink into this register of ASTRA objects: the findings
that back each result, the decisions that configured the pipeline, and the
input/output product tables. Prior-literature insights need no visible register —
each inline {astra:prior-insight} reference and each decision option carries its
claim and source on hover, and the plugin emits the hidden hover targets those
references resolve against automatically.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
hierarchy.lvl3: 1. The BAO feature in ξ(s) visibly sharpens from pre- to post-reconstruction across tracers.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
hierarchy.lvl3: 1. The BAO feature in ξ(s) visibly sharpens from pre- to post-reconstruction across tracers.

records.content:
The isolated-feature curves show a narrower, taller peak near
100 Mpc/h post-recon than pre-recon, consistent with
displacement-field reconstruction undoing the non-linear bulk
flows that smear the acoustic signature.

Scope: LRG1/LRG2/LRG3/ELG1/ELG2/LRG3+ELG1, baseline universe.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
hierarchy.lvl3: 2. The acoustic feature is detected post-reconstruction in every tracer.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
hierarchy.lvl3: 2. The acoustic feature is detected post-reconstruction in every tracer.

records.content:
Δχ²(α_iso) profiles comparing the damped-BAO template to a
no-BAO reference show a well-defined minimum near α_iso ≈ 1
in each case.

Scope: LRG1, LRG2, LRG3, ELG1, ELG2, LRG3+ELG1 — post-reconstruction, baseline universe.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
hierarchy.lvl3: 3. Reconstruction shrinks σ(α_iso) across every fitted tracer.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
hierarchy.lvl3: 3. Reconstruction shrinks σ(α_iso) across every fitted tracer.

records.content:
σ(α_iso) falls by a factor of ~1.4–1.8 across the 2D-fit tracers
(LRG1 0.0167 → 0.0114, LRG2 0.0198 → 0.0113, LRG3 0.0131 →
0.0093) and by ~2.9× for the 1D ELG1 fit (0.0772 → 0.0266).

Scope: LRG1/LRG2/LRG3/ELG1 under the baseline universe.

ELG1 20.4/19. The damped-BAO template plus spline broadband
adequately describes the measured correlation functions over

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Covariance matrix source — selected: DESI DR1 published RascalC-rescaled (fiducial)

The ξ covariance for every BAO fit comes from RascalC
(semi-analytic 4-point integral + jackknife shot-noise rescaling).
RascalC is calibrated against the DESI fiducial reconstruction +
clustering pipeline: IterativeFFT RecSym, Σ_sm=15, lin4 s-binning,
WEIGHT*WEIGHT_FKP weights, and the specific random density used in
the published runs. Choosing the published VAC cov therefore locks
downstream recon + clustering decisions to the fiducial options;
the self_computed alternative was considered
(misharash/RascalC-scripts + Misha’s RascalC C++ fork on NERSC)
but is excluded as out of scope for this reproduction -- see
excluded_reason.

Ingested from /global/cfs/cdirs/desi/users/mrash/RascalC/Y1/
unblinded/v1.2/. One cov per tracer × pre/post state.

Full RascalC pipeline (pair counting + 4-point integration +
jackknife rescaling) against DR1 catalogs, per
misharash/RascalC-scripts.

~20 node-days of compute across all tracers × pre/post, plus
a private desi_y1_files (cosmodesi/desi-y1-kp) dependency
for filename resolution, and a version-drift issue (scripts
target v1.5, published cov is v1.2). Not in scope for this
reproduction. Choosing this option would also require
unlocking non-fiducial recon + clustering decisions (not
currently exposed as options).

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Reconstruction Gaussian smoothing scale (BGS/LRG/ELG) — selected: 15 Mpc/h (fiducial)

Smoothing scale Sigma_sm applied to the density field before solving
for the displacement, for the BGS / LRG / ELG parents.
The BAO model’s damping correction uses the same value, so recon and
fit must agree -- that’s why this lives at the root and recon inherits
it. QSO has a distinct fiducial (sm30 vs sm15 here) and is
parameterized separately via smoothing_radius_qso.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Reconstruction Gaussian smoothing scale (QSO only) — selected: 30 Mpc/h (fiducial)

QSO’s fiducial reconstruction smoothing is 30 h⁻¹ Mpc (vs 15 h⁻¹ Mpc
for BGS/LRG/ELG), reflecting the QSO sample’s lower number density
and larger coherence scale. The BAO model’s damping template must
match, so both the recon script and the fit scripts branch on
tracer.id == "qso" to consume this decision rather than
smoothing_radius. sm20 is the sensitivity-test alternative for QSO.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Broadband model — selected: Spline (power, fiducial)

Spline-based broadband (fiducial) vs polynomial-based (legacy form).
They give consistent BAO scales; the small difference goes into the
systematic error budget.

desilike broadband=‘power’.

Supporting insight: 

DESI adopts a cubic-spline-based broadband model as fiducial, replacing the polynomial broadband used by previous (e)BOSS analyses, because the spline method has stronger theoretical motivation and is less arbitrary in its choice of free parameters.

desilike broadband=‘poly’ (ξ: Σ a_ℓ,n s^-n).

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
BAO damping parameter prior — selected: Gaussian (fiducial)

Gaussian priors on Σ_perp, Σ_par, Σ_s centered at fiducial values.
Gaussian is more robust than flat priors, but results are consistent
either way.

Normal priors at fiducial centres.

Supporting insights: 

Tight Gaussian priors (width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc) on the BAO damping parameters centered on theory values are recommended; fully fixing them risks biases and flat priors can also bias α and weaken constraints on noisy data., 

Biases of order 0.1% in α_iso and 0.2% in α_AP appear when the damping parameters are misspecified by more than ~2 h^-1 Mpc from their fiducial values, justifying Gaussian priors of width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc., 

Σ⊥ and Σ∥ follow Σ⊥ = Σ0·G, Σ∥ = Σ0·G·(1+f) where Σ0 = 12.4 h^-1 Mpc for σ8 = 0.9 at z=0, scaling linearly with σ8 and redshift via the growth function -- the theoretical prediction that anchors the Gaussian prior centres., 

Nonlinear erasure of the BAO is modelled as convolution with a Gaussian Lagrangian-displacement distribution, which Fourier-transforms to the multiplicative exp(-k²Σ²/2) damping form used in BAO template fits., 

Because Lagrangian displacements are dominated by bulk flows, galaxy bias has only a small effect on Σ⊥/Σ∥: highly biased tracers increase Lagrangian variances only by small amounts. Supports tracer-common Σ priors with modest tracer-dependent offsets.

Uniform priors U[0, 20] for each damping parameter.

Supporting insight: 

Tight Gaussian priors (width 1-2 h^-1 Mpc) on the BAO damping parameters centered on theory values are recommended; fully fixing them risks biases and flat priors can also bias α and weaken constraints on noisy data.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Damping prior central values — selected: Fiducial (per tracer)

Fiducial Σ_perp, Σ_par, Σ_s per tracer × recon state.
The per-tracer centers live in scripts/tracers.py; this decision is the
multiplicative scaling applied to them. Variants widen / narrow the
centers as a systematic test.

Unscaled fiducial centers for the tracer being fit.

Supporting insights: 

Σ⊥ and Σ∥ follow Σ⊥ = Σ0·G, Σ∥ = Σ0·G·(1+f) where Σ0 = 12.4 h^-1 Mpc for σ8 = 0.9 at z=0, scaling linearly with σ8 and redshift via the growth function -- the theoretical prediction that anchors the Gaussian prior centres., 

Because Lagrangian displacements are dominated by bulk flows, galaxy bias has only a small effect on Σ⊥/Σ∥: highly biased tracers increase Lagrangian variances only by small amounts. Supports tracer-common Σ priors with modest tracer-dependent offsets.

Multiply fiducial centers by 1.5.

Multiply fiducial centers by 0.67.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Wiggle / no-wiggle split — selected: Peak-average (Brieden+2022, fiducial)

Fiducial uses the peak-average method of Brieden, Gil-Marín & Verde
2022 to split P_linear into wiggle + smooth components.
Eisenstein-Hu 1998 and Savitzky-Golay log-space filters are the
historical alternatives. The systematic budget assigns < 0.02% α_iso
/ < 0.09% α_AP to this choice. Only the fiducial is wired.

desilike default for BAOPowerSpectrumTemplate.

Supporting insight: 

The DESI fiducial BAO template is constructed using the Wallisch (2018) peak-averaging/dewiggling method; previous BOSS/eBOSS analyses used varying methods (Eisenstein-Hu, Kirkby, etc.).

Legacy analytic fit; not wired.

Supporting insight: 

The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred.

Polynomial smoother; not wired.

Supporting insight: 

The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
FoG damping placement — selected: Smooth-only (fiducial)

Σ_s multiplies the smooth (no-wiggle) component only; the legacy
approach applied it to the full template, which introduces a
degeneracy with the BAO damping. “smooth_only” is the fiducial,
built into desilike’s DampedBAOWigglesTracer... model;
the “full_model” alternative is declared but not wired
(would need a custom theory wrapper).

Σ_s multiplies P_nw only; desilike default.

Supporting insights: 

The fiducial DESI choice applies the Fingers-of-God (Σ_s) damping only to the smooth model component and not to the BAO wiggle component; previous models applied FoG to both, and the fiducial decoupling makes BAO damping interpretation simpler., 

Including the FoG damping only in the smooth component removes a clear degeneracy between the BAO (Σ_||) damping and the FoG (Σ_s) damping scales, making prior enforcement and convergence easier.

Σ_s multiplies wiggle + smooth; not wired, raises NotImplementedError.

Supporting insight: 

Placing the FoG damping on the wiggle component in addition to the smooth component shifts α_AP by an average of 0.11% (a 2σ-detected systematic) and α_iso by ≤ 0.02%, motivating inclusion of this effect in the modelling systematic budget.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Component that α dilates — selected: Wiggle-only (fiducial)

The α_iso / α_AP rescaling is applied to the wiggle component only;
the smooth P_nw is evaluated at observed coordinates without dilation.
Dilating the full template is the alternative; the systematic budget
assigns < 0.02% α_iso / < 0.09% α_AP to this choice. Only the
fiducial is wired.

α rescales P_w; P_nw at observed coords. desilike default.

Supporting insight: 

DESI fiducial practice dilates only the BAO wiggle component by α, not the full template; previous analyses dilated both smooth and wiggle components, and dilating only wiggles guarantees that α captures only BAO information.

α rescales wiggle + smooth; not wired, raises NotImplementedError.

Supporting insight: 

Whether the smooth component is dilated in addition to the wiggle component induces a modelling systematic of < 0.02% on α_iso and < 0.09% on α_AP.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
BAO template cosmology — selected: c000 -- Planck 2018 ΛCDM (fiducial)

AbacusSummit cosmologies used to test fiducial-cosmology systematics.
c000 is Planck-2018 ΛCDM (fiducial).

Supporting insights: 

Planck 2018 TT,TE,EE+lowE+lensing base-ΛCDM gives Ω_b h² = 0.02237, Ω_c h² = 0.1200, n_s = 0.9649, H0 = 67.36 km/s/Mpc, Ω_m = 0.3153 -- the values adopted as AbacusSummit c000., 

Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM constrains the sound horizon at the drag epoch to r_drag = 147.09 ± 0.26 Mpc, the CMB anchor for the BAO standard ruler., 

Planck 2018 base-ΛCDM assumes three neutrino species approximated as two massless and a single massive neutrino of mass m_ν = 0.06 eV; Ω_m includes this neutrino contribution., 

Planck 2018 + BAO constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with the Standard Model N_eff = 3.046 -- showing AbacusSummit c003 (N_eff = 3.70) is well outside the Planck preferred range.





Supporting insight: 

Planck 2018 + BAO constrains N_eff = 2.99 ± 0.17, consistent with the Standard Model N_eff = 3.046 -- showing AbacusSummit c003 (N_eff = 3.70) is well outside the Planck preferred range.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Reported error includes modelling systematic — selected: Include modelling budget (headline)

Combined modelling systematic (0.1% α_iso, 0.2% α_AP, conservatively
0.2% on α_⊥ / α_∥) added in quadrature to the reported σ. Consumed
only by make_alpha_table.py -- the MCMC chains themselves are
untouched, so both options can be produced from the same fits by
re-running the table step. The stat-only σ is always kept as a
companion column (alpha{1,2}_std_stat).

σ_reported = hypot(σ_stat, frac·α).

Supporting insights: 

Summing theory and modelling systematics in quadrature, the recommended combined systematic budget for DESI post-reconstruction BAO is 0.1% on α_iso and 0.2% on α_AP., 

Placing the FoG damping on the wiggle component in addition to the smooth component shifts α_AP by an average of 0.11% (a 2σ-detected systematic) and α_iso by ≤ 0.02%, motivating inclusion of this effect in the modelling systematic budget., 

The choice of wiggle/no-wiggle split method (Eisenstein-Hu, Hinton+2017, Wallisch, Brieden+2022, Savitzky-Golay) introduces a maximum modelling systematic of only 0.02% on α_iso and 0.09% on α_AP under the fiducial fitting methodology, with no single method theoretically preferred., 

Whether the smooth component is dilated in addition to the wiggle component induces a modelling systematic of < 0.02% on α_iso and < 0.09% on α_AP.

σ_reported = σ_stat; tests raw constraining power.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Fit range in s — selected: [48, 152] Mpc/h  (fiducial)

Fiducial is s ∈ [48, 152] Mpc/h at 4 Mpc/h binning.
Extend/narrow as sensitivity tests.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Multipoles fit (LRG only; 1D tracers override) — selected: ℓ = 0, 2  (2D, LRG default)

LRG bins are fit with 2D (ℓ=0, 2). ELG1 / QSO are 1D-only regardless
of this setting -- the tracer’s ells in scripts/tracers.py caps
the universe choice via an intersection in
bao_utils.resolve_settings, so twod means “2D where supported”.

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
BAO fit method — selected: Sampling (emcee MCMC, fiducial)

Fiducial reports posterior-based intervals for the combined-tracer
rows and headline cosmological parameters — those come from emcee
chains. Profiling (Minuit) gives a faster point estimate +
parabolic errors, useful as a pipeline sanity check but not the
fiducial for reported alphas.

Full posterior via emcee; burn-in + thin; matches DESI Y1v1 chains.

MLE + Hessian intervals; fast sanity check.

Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Inputs consumed by the analysis:

Input

Type

Description

desi_dr1_lss_catalogs

data

DESI DR1 iron v1.5 LSS clustering catalogs (data + 18 randoms per cap).
Used by reconstruction (raw catalogs) and clustering (pre-recon xi).
All tracers live here -- LRG_{NGC,SGC}clustering.{dat,ran_i}.fits,
ELG_LOPnotqso, QSO_.

desi_fiducial_cosmology

data

Tabulated z -> r(z) for the DESI fiducial cosmology
(AbacusSummit c000, Planck 2018 LCDM, Omega_m=0.3153, h=0.6736).
Must be used for redshift -> distance conversion to match DESI’s
pkrun.py / TabulatedDESI; otherwise window/covariance get inflated.

desi_rascalc_cov_bgs

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for BGS_BRIGHT-21.5, GCcomb, z=0.1-0.4,
post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm15). Consumed by the BGS post-recon BAO fit
(1D monopole fit; ~4σ detection).

desi_rascalc_cov_bgs_pre

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for BGS_BRIGHT-21.5, GCcomb, z=0.1-0.4,
PRE-recon. Used for the BGS pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg1

data

DESI pre-computed RascalC analytic covariance for LRG, GCcomb,
z=0.4-0.6, post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm15), default+FKP weights,
lin4 binning, s=20-200 Mpc/h. ASCII matrix.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg1_pre

data

DESI pre-computed RascalC analytic covariance for LRG, GCcomb,
z=0.4-0.6, PRE-recon, default+FKP weights, lin4 binning, s=20-200 Mpc/h.
Used for the pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg2

data

DESI pre-computed RascalC analytic covariance for LRG, GCcomb,
z=0.6-0.8, post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm15). Same format as
desi_rascalc_cov_lrg1.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg2_pre

data

DESI pre-computed RascalC analytic covariance for LRG, GCcomb,
z=0.6-0.8, PRE-recon. Used for the pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg3

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for LRG, GCcomb, z=0.8-1.1, post-recon
(IFFT recsym, sm15). Consumed by the LRG3 post-recon BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg3_pre

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for LRG, GCcomb, z=0.8-1.1, PRE-recon.
Used for the LRG3 pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_elg1

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for ELG_LOPnotqso, GCcomb, z=0.8-1.1,
post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm15). Consumed by the ELG1 post-recon BAO fit
(1D monopole fit).

desi_rascalc_cov_elg1_pre

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for ELG_LOPnotqso, GCcomb, z=0.8-1.1,
PRE-recon. Used for the ELG1 pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_elg2

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for ELG_LOPnotqso, GCcomb, z=1.1-1.6,
post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm15). Consumed by the ELG2 post-recon BAO fit
(2D monopole + quadrupole).

desi_rascalc_cov_elg2_pre

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for ELG_LOPnotqso, GCcomb, z=1.1-1.6,
PRE-recon. Used for the ELG2 pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg3_elg1

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for the LRG+ELG_LOPnotqso combined
tracer, GCcomb, z=0.8-1.1, post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm15). Consumed
by the LRG3+ELG1 combined-tracer fit (headline row).

desi_rascalc_cov_qso

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for QSO, GCcomb, z=0.8-2.1,
post-recon (IFFT recsym, sm30 — fiducial smoothing for QSO).
Consumed by the QSO post-recon BAO fit (1D monopole fit;
~4.2σ detection).

desi_rascalc_cov_qso_pre

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for QSO, GCcomb, z=0.8-2.1,
PRE-recon. Used for the QSO pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

desi_rascalc_cov_lrg3_elg1_pre

data

DESI RascalC analytic covariance for the LRG+ELG_LOPnotqso combined
tracer, GCcomb, z=0.8-1.1, PRE-recon. Used for the combined-tracer
pre-reconstruction BAO fit.

Final BAO distance constraints D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d, D_V/r_d, D_M/D_H per
post-recon tracer with systematic budget folded in. 1D tracers
(BGS, ELG1, QSO) report only D_V/r_d.

distance_residuals_plot

figure

Corner plot of post-recon BAO distance posteriors with α residuals
from fiducial Planck 2018-ΛCDM in a top inset.

hubble_diagram_plot

figure

BAO Hubble diagram of D_M/r_d, D_H/r_d, D_V/r_d, D_M/D_H ratios to
fiducial Planck 2018-ΛCDM; DESI DR1 stars overlaid on prior surveys
(6dFGS, WiggleZ, SDSS DR16, DES Y6).

hierarchy.lvl1: Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1
Configuration-Space BAO Distances from DESI DR1

records.content:
Density-field reconstruction for DESI DR1 parent samples — one pass per parent (BGS 0.1–0.4, LRGs 0.4–1.1, ELGs 0.8–1.6, QSO 0.8–2.1), emitting parent-level shifted galaxy + random catalogs. Per-z-bin slicing is deferred to clustering, so the reconstruction solve sees the full parent redshift range; the shifted catalogs are then cut into tracer z-bins downstream at ξ-time.

Two-point correlation function ξ(s, μ) for DESI DR1 galaxy tracers. Per-tracer fan-out: xi_{pre,post}_recon_<tracer>. Composite tracers (LRG3+ELG1) union multiple parent NPZs and z-slice their catalogs at ξ time.



Chen et al. (2024)

Seo & Eisenstein (2007)

Planck Collaboration et al. (2018)

Eisenstein et al. (2006)

Padmanabhan et al. (2008)

hierarchy.lvl1: Reconstruction
Reconstruction

records.content:
Sub-analysis page. The astra:* components below are scoped into the
reconstruction sub-analysis with the reconstruction. path prefix.

hierarchy.lvl1: Reconstruction
Reconstruction

records.content:
Density-field reconstruction runs first in the pipeline. The linear
displacement field Ψ is estimated from the Gaussian-smoothed raw density and
applied symmetrically to galaxies and randoms (RecSym), sharpening the BAO peak
before clustering measures ξ(s). One pass per parent sample (BGS, LRG, ELG, QSO)
emits parent-level shifted catalogs; per-z-bin slicing is deferred to the


clustering stage.

hierarchy.lvl1: Reconstruction
Reconstruction

records.content:
The reconstruction algorithm is IterativeFFT (fiducial), with MultiGrid exposed
as a sensitivity alternative:

hierarchy.lvl1: Reconstruction
Reconstruction

records.content:
Reconstruction algorithm — selected: IterativeFFT (pyrecon)

Fiducial choice is IterativeFFT. MultiGrid is the main
alternative; results are consistent (HOD-level).

Iterative FFT-based reconstruction (fiducial).

Supporting insights: 

Density-field reconstruction sharpens the acoustic peak in the correlation function (equivalently, restores higher harmonics in the power spectrum) -- the core motivation for applying reconstruction before BAO measurement., 

Nonlinear structure formation and redshift-space distortions displace galaxies from their initial positions, blurring the BAO peak at ~150 Mpc and erasing higher harmonics in P(k)., 

Nonlinear smearing reduces the precision of the BAO distance scale achievable per unit survey volume by roughly a factor of three at the present day., 

Reconstruction is particularly effective at low redshift where nonlinearities are worst -- exactly the regime most sensitive to dark energy., 

At z=0.3, reconstruction can reduce the sample-variance error bar on the acoustic scale by at least a factor of 2 and in principle nearly a factor of 4., 

The simple linear-theory reconstruction in this paper improves distance precision from 1.4%/1.9% to 0.75%/0.95% in real/redshift space at z=0.3 -- the canonical factor-of-two gain., 

Reconstruction is easier at higher redshifts because raw displacements are smaller there -- corollary: reconstruction is most needed, and provides the largest gains, at low redshift., 

Overall conclusion: density-field reconstruction offers a factor-of-2-to-3 improvement to the BAO method, particularly at lower redshifts., 

Reconstruction reduces the non-linear damping of the BAO oscillations by about a factor of two when the input density field is smoothed on the non-linear scale., 

Within the Lagrangian/Zel'dovich framework, the linear-power-spectrum contribution to the reconstructed power spectrum is less damped than in the non-linear power spectrum -- the core theoretical reason reconstruction sharpens the BAO peak., 

Reconstruction also reduces the mode-coupling terms that introduce out-of-phase oscillation components and shift the acoustic peak -- complementary to the damping-reduction motivation.

Alternative; near-identical BAO scales.

The displacement convention is RecSym — both data and randoms are shifted, which
is what keeps the post-reconstruction ξ estimator unbiased:

hierarchy.lvl1: Reconstruction
Reconstruction

records.content:
Reconstruction convention — selected: RecSym

RecSym shifts both data and randoms by the redshift-space displacement
(fiducial -- physically motivated, simpler BAO damping).
RecIso is the legacy convention (data shifted by full
displacement, randoms by real-space-only).

Symmetric shifts of data + randoms (fiducial).

Supporting insights: 

The conceptual basis of reconstruction is to move measured densities (not all particles) back to their initial location -- established by shifting a reference grid of smoothly distributed particles alongside the real particles and differencing the densities., 

The seminal recon algorithm shifts a reference grid of smoothly distributed particles (i.e., randoms) by the same displacement field as the real particles -- the operational prescription behind RecSym., 

The reconstructed density field is defined as the difference between the shifted real-particle density and the shifted reference-particle density -- the symmetric convention that displaces both tracers and randoms.

Legacy convention; isotropic-only random shift.

The Gaussian smoothing scale is not decided here: it is inherited from the root
via from: ../smoothing_radius, so the Σ_sm used in the displacement solve and
the Σ_sm in the downstream BAO damping template stay identical by construction.

hierarchy.lvl1: Reconstruction
Reconstruction

records.content:
Reconstruction emits a shifted galaxy + random catalog per parent sample,
alongside diagnostic mean-displacement metrics:

Output

Type

Description

post_recon_catalog_bgs_full

data

Shifted galaxy + random catalogs after reconstruction, BGS parent sample
(z=0.1-0.4, BGS_BRIGHT-21.5). Same NPZ format as
post_recon_catalog_lrg_full. The BGS tracer uses the full parent range
(not sub-binned).

mean_displacement_bgs_full

metric

Mean |Psi| for BGS parent (Mpc/h).

post_recon_catalog_lrg_full

data

Shifted galaxy + random catalogs after reconstruction, LRG parent sample
(z=0.4-1.1). NPZ with arrays: gal_pos, gal_pos_shift, gal_weights, gal_z,
ran_pos, ran_pos_shift, ran_weights, ran_z (NGC+SGC concatenated;
positions in Cartesian Mpc/h, DESI fiducial cosmology). Downstream xi
computation slices by tracer z-range using gal_z / ran_z.

mean_displacement_lrg_full

metric

Mean |Psi| for LRG parent (Mpc/h). Typically 5-10 Mpc/h for LRGs.

post_recon_catalog_qso_full

data

Shifted galaxy + random catalogs after reconstruction, QSO parent sample
(z=0.8-2.1). Same NPZ format as post_recon_catalog_lrg_full. QSO uses
Sigma_sm = 30 h^-1 Mpc rather than 15 h^-1 Mpc.

mean_displacement_qso_full

metric

Mean |Psi| for QSO parent (Mpc/h).

post_recon_catalog_elg_full

data

Shifted galaxy + random catalogs after reconstruction, ELG parent sample
(z=0.8-1.6). Same format as post_recon_catalog_lrg_full. Sliced
downstream into ELG1 (0.8-1.1) and (later) ELG2 (1.1-1.6).

mean_displacement_elg_full

metric

Mean |Psi| for ELG parent (Mpc/h).
